This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-08T23:01:36.9475822Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T22:59:15.790876353Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get a specific label from a repository."},"description":"Get a specific label from a repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"name":{"description":"Label name.","type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner (username or organization name)","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo","name"],"type":"object"},"name":"get_label"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get issue details"},"description":"Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"issue_number":{"description":"The number of the issue","type":"number"},"method":{"description":"The read operation to perform on a single issue.\nOptions are:\n1. get - Get details of a specific issue.\n2. get_comments - Get issue comments.\n3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.\n4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.\n","enum":["get","get_comments","get_sub_issues","get_labels"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"The owner of the repository","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"The name of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"issue_read"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List available issue types"},"description":"List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"The organization owner of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["owner"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issue_types"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List issues"},"description":"List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"after":{"description":"Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. 2026-04-08T23:01:36.9574836Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T23:00:16.435428503Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57954,\"title\":\"[ConfidentialLedger] Docs: Document redirect policy behavior and complete Examples section in README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] by `@ryazhang-microsoft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57869 significantly refactored `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` to cache the primary node URL from redirect responses and reuse it for subsequent non-GET requests. This improves write operation performance on multi-node ACL clusters. The policy was originally introduced in 1.4.1-beta.3 and improved in 1.4.1-beta.5. Neither the original introduction nor the caching improvement is reflected in the README.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`## Examples` section says \\u0026#34;Coming Soon...\\u0026#34;** — despite the `Key concepts` section already containing inline code snippets, there is no organized Examples section with runnable, snippet-backed samples\\n- **`ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` is undocumented** — the automatic 307/308 redirect following (with Authorization header preservation) is a significant behavioral feature that users migrating from earlier versions or other SDKs need to know about; no README section explains this\\n- **`VerifyConnection` default change is undocumented** — beta.4 changed the default for `VerifyConnection` in `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions` from `false` to `true`; this breaking-by-intent change has no README guidance\\n- **API reference documentation link is commented out** — `` is commented out in the header badge row\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n#### 1a. Add a \\u0026#34;Redirect handling\\u0026#34; subsection under `Key concepts`\\n\\nInsert the following after the `### Confidential consortium and enclave verifications` section and before `### Thread safety`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Redirect handling\\n\\nAzure Confidential Ledger is a distributed system with multiple nodes. Write operations (non-GET requests) must be routed to the primary node; a non-primary node returns an HTTP 307/308 redirect to the current primary. The SDK automatically handles these redirects while preserving the `Authorization` header (which standard HTTP clients strip on cross-domain redirects).\\n\\nFor efficiency, the SDK caches the most recently observed primary node URL and uses it as the first target for subsequent write requests, reducing the number of redirects experienced during steady-state operation. The cache is refreshed automatically whenever the service redirects to a new primary node.\\n\\nThis behavior is built into `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`, which is added to the pipeline by default. No extra configuration is required.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Replace the `## Examples` placeholder\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nComing Soon...\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Append an entry and verify it was committed\\n\\n```\\nOperation postOperation = ledgerClient.PostLedgerEntry(\\n    waitUntil: WaitUntil.Completed,\\n    RequestContent.Create(\\n        new { contents = \\u0026#34;Hello world!\\u0026#34; }));\\n\\nstring transactionId = postOperation.Id;\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Appended transaction with Id: {transactionId}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Check transaction status\\n\\n```\\nResponse statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId);\\n\\nstring status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content)\\n    .RootElement\\n    .GetProperty(\\u0026#34;state\\u0026#34;)\\n    .GetString();\\n\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Transaction status: {status}\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n// Wait for the entry to be committed\\nwhile (status == \\u0026#34;Pending\\u0026#34;)\\n{\\n    statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId);\\n    status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content)\\n        .RootElement\\n        .GetProperty(\\u0026#34;state\\u0026#34;)\\n        .GetString();\\n}\\n\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Transaction status: {status}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Get a cryptographic receipt\\n\\n```\\nResponse receiptResponse = ledgerClient.GetReceipt(transactionId);\\nstring receiptJson = new StreamReader(receiptResponse.ContentStream).ReadToEnd();\\n\\nConsole.WriteLine(receiptJson);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** The snippet names (`AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt`) are already defined in the existing Key concepts section. Confirm the snippet test file exists at `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/` — if the `#region Snippet:` tags are present there, the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool will fill in the code automatically. If not, the snippet regions should be added to the test file.\\n\\n#### 1c. Document `VerifyConnection` default in the \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; section\\n\\nAdd under `#### Using Azure Active Directory`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** By default, `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions.VerifyConnection` is `true`, which means the client validates the TLS certificate of the ledger node it connects to. Set this to `false` only in test or development environments.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippets `AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt` should already exist in the Key concepts section of the README and in a test/sample file. Verify:\\n\\n1. Check `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/` for existing `#region Snippet:` tags for `AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, and `GetReceipt`.\\n2. If not present, create `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` with the sample code wrapped in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` blocks.\\n3. Add the snippet placeholders in README under `## Examples` as shown above.\\n4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` to inject the code.\\n\\nReference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` must contain these sections in this order. Current state:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started / Install the package | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started / Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started / Authenticate the client | ✅ Present (minor gap: no VerifyConnection note) |\\n| Key concepts | ✅ Present (but missing redirect policy) |\\n| Examples | ❌ \\u0026#34;Coming Soon...\\u0026#34; — needs real content |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` — confirms snippets are injected from test files into README\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm API listings are current\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
